topStories.title: Global Market: China's upbeat trade data lifts hopes for export-linked stocks
topStories.summary: China's exports saw a strong 27% year-on-year increase in June. Imports also climbed a significant 36%, accelerating from May's figures. This performance indicates resilient global demand and strengthening domestic economic activity. The robust trade data suggests improved industrial output and consumption within China. These positive figures are expected to boost investor confidence in Chinese markets.

topStories.title: Axis Securities picks 6 small, midcap stocks that can rally up to 36%. How many are in your portfolio?
topStories.summary: Axis Securities has identified six promising small and midcap stocks for investors. These selected companies offer potential rallies ranging up to thirty-six percent. Ujjivan Small Finance Bank and Chalet Hotels are among the featured selections. Minda Corporation and Healthcare Global Enterprises also present strong growth prospects. Dalmia Bharat and APL Apollo Tubes complete the list of recommended stocks.

topStories.title: Andy Burnham set to be U.K. PM after securing more Labour MPs' support
topStories.summary: After receiving the votes of 322 of 403 Labour MPs on the first day of nominations on July 9, he had gained an extra 27 nominations by July 13 afternoon

topStories.title: Gold prices snap 2-day fall, silver at Rs 2.18 lakh/kg despite rising oil prices, rate hike fears. Key levels to watch today
topStories.summary: Gold and silver prices rebounded on MCX on Tuesday, snapping a two-session losing streak, as escalating US-Iran tensions boosted safe-haven demand. Investors are now awaiting the U.S. June CPI data for fresh cues on inflation and the Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory.

topStories.title: Laser Power & Infra IPO allotment expected today; GMP signals 19% listing premium. Here's how to check your status
topStories.summary: Laser Power & Infra IPO allotment status is expected today for the Rs 742-crore public issue. The company's shares are scheduled to debut on July 16, 2026, on both exchanges. The IPO witnessed an enthusiastic response, receiving an overall subscription of 38.94 times. Its grey market premium stands at 19%, signalling expectations of a healthy listing gain. Proceeds will primarily prepay outstanding borrowings and fund general corporate purposes.

topStories.title: ICICI Prudential AMC shares gain 2% after Q1 net profit rises 23% to Rs 965 crore. Here’s what brokerages say
topStories.summary: ICICI Prudential AMC reported a 23% net profit increase for Q1 FY27.  Revenue from operations also saw an 18% year-on-year growth.  Mutual fund assets under management rose by 11% to Rs 82.40 lakh crore.  Brokerages like JM Financial and Motilal Oswal maintained positive ratings.  Emkay also reiterated its buy recommendation with a higher target price.

topStories.title: HCL Tech shares fall 3% after Q1 results. What Nomura, Motilal Oswal & other brokerages suggest now
topStories.summary: HCL Tech shares fell over 3% despite reporting a 20% YoY rise in Q1FY27 consolidated net profit to Rs 4,624 crore and a 13% increase in revenue to Rs 34,579 crore. The IT major retained its FY27 constant currency guidance, forecasting 1-4% revenue growth, 1.5-4.5% services revenue growth, and an EBIT margin of 17.5-18.5%, disappointing investors.

topStories.title: Adityanath flags environmental concerns, launches 35-crore sapling drive
topStories.summary: CM said that at Prime Minister Narendra Modi's request, under the 'Ek Pedh Maa Ke Naam' campaign, 5 crore saplings were planted this year on June 5, which is World Environment Day. More than 35 crore saplings were planted last year, bringing the total to 242 crore saplings planted in the state over the past nine years.

topStories.title: A $2.5 billion FII buying has domestic investors asking: Is foreign money shifting to India?
topStories.summary: Foreign investors bought Indian equities in July after significant selling periods. This return follows easing geopolitical concerns and improved global risk sentiment. India's growth story and diversified opportunities attract foreign capital for the long term. Analysts remain cautious, awaiting sustained inflows to confirm a trend. Crude oil prices and US interest rates will influence future foreign investment decisions.

topStories.title: Theft attempt at Canara Bank branch in Farangipet
topStories.summary: Bantwal Rural police said the thieves entered the branch, located in the Menezes Plaza complex, by breaking open a portion of the roof at night

topStories.title: Sensex falls 500 points, Nifty slips below 24,100 as US-Iran conflict escalates. What lies ahead?
topStories.summary: Indian stock markets edged lower on Tuesday, with major indices Sensex and Nifty falling. Escalating Middle East tensions contributed to rising oil prices, impacting investor sentiment negatively. Several prominent companies like HCL Technologies and Bajaj Finance saw significant share price drops. While some sectors like IT and Metal showed marginal gains, the overall market breadth remained bearish.

topStories.title: Grasim shares in focus after subsidiary acquires Solenergi Power from Shell in $1.8 billion deal
topStories.summary: Grasim Industries' renewable energy arm will acquire Solenergi Power from Shell. This acquisition significantly expands its clean energy portfolio to 9.4 GW. The deal, valued at Rs 17,200 crore, is expected to close by 2026. Funding will come from debt, equity, and an investment by Global Infrastructure Partners. The combined entity aims for 20 GW of renewable capacity within three years.

topStories.title: Q1 Showdown: Analysts pick top bets as ICICI Bank, HDFC, Axis, Kotak, Yes Bank gear up for results this week
topStories.summary: Five major private banks will announce their Q1 results this Saturday. Analysts offer mixed views on which banking stocks investors should consider. ICICI Bank is favored for its strong performance and reasonable valuation. HDFC Bank is seen as a defensive choice, while Axis Bank shows momentum. Kotak Mahindra Bank and Yes Bank present more selective investment opportunities.

topStories.title: Rupee drops past 96 per dollar as oil prices climb on renewed US-Iran tensions
topStories.summary: The rupee weakened past 96 against the dollar on Tuesday. This decline occurred as oil prices saw a sharp increase this week. Tensions in the Middle East deepened, leading to fresh strikes between the US and Iran. Indian equities also faced pressure, with the Nifty 50 index experiencing losses. The rupee reached its weakest point since late May due to these combined factors.

NTPC and Power Grid Corporation are also highlighted for their performance.

topStories.title: Global Market Update | SK Hynix set to gain as South Korea eases capital-raising rules
topStories.summary: South Korea's ruling party seeks to ease rules for SK Hynix joint ventures.  This move aims to attract outside capital for new chip factories.  The proposed law change allows subsidiaries to form ventures with investors.  SK Hynix needs significant funds for its aggressive chip expansion plans.  New ventures must also locate outside the Seoul metropolitan area.

topStories.title: ETMarkets Management Talk | We want to grow 20% annually without taking on debt: Bansal Wire's Pranav Bansal
topStories.summary: Bansal Wire Industries is focusing on operational efficiency and cash generation for growth. The company targets twenty percent annual growth without increasing leverage. This growth will be achieved through stronger free cash flows and internal accruals. Bansal Wire Industries is expanding into speciality wires and adopting AI-driven manufacturing. Improving return on capital and reducing debt remain central to its roadmap.

topStories.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | Equity, mutual funds, bonds or property: Tax rules every NRI should know: Ritu Shaktawat
topStories.summary: NRIs investing in India must understand tax rules and residential status.  Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements offer beneficial tax treatments for certain income streams.  Recent changes affect capital gains and mutual fund taxation significantly for investors.  Careful planning of investment exits and documentation is crucial for compliance.  Adhering to FEMA and RBI regulations ensures smooth repatriation of funds.

business.title: Global Market: China's upbeat trade data lifts hopes for export-linked stocks
business.summary: China's exports saw a strong 27% year-on-year increase in June. Imports also climbed a significant 36%, accelerating from May's figures. This performance indicates resilient global demand and strengthening domestic economic activity. The robust trade data suggests improved industrial output and consumption within China. These positive figures are expected to boost investor confidence in Chinese markets.

business.title: Laser Power & Infra IPO allotment expected today; GMP signals 19% listing premium. Here's how to check your status
business.summary: Laser Power & Infra IPO allotment status is expected today for the Rs 742-crore public issue. The company's shares are scheduled to debut on July 16, 2026, on both exchanges. The IPO witnessed an enthusiastic response, receiving an overall subscription of 38.94 times. Its grey market premium stands at 19%, signalling expectations of a healthy listing gain. Proceeds will primarily prepay outstanding borrowings and fund general corporate purposes.

business.title: ICICI Prudential AMC shares gain 2% after Q1 net profit rises 23% to Rs 965 crore. Here’s what brokerages say
business.summary: ICICI Prudential AMC reported a 23% net profit increase for Q1 FY27.  Revenue from operations also saw an 18% year-on-year growth.  Mutual fund assets under management rose by 11% to Rs 82.40 lakh crore.  Brokerages like JM Financial and Motilal Oswal maintained positive ratings.  Emkay also reiterated its buy recommendation with a higher target price.

business.title: HCL Tech shares fall 3% after Q1 results. What Nomura, Motilal Oswal & other brokerages suggest now
business.summary: HCL Tech shares fell over 3% despite reporting a 20% YoY rise in Q1FY27 consolidated net profit to Rs 4,624 crore and a 13% increase in revenue to Rs 34,579 crore. The IT major retained its FY27 constant currency guidance, forecasting 1-4% revenue growth, 1.5-4.5% services revenue growth, and an EBIT margin of 17.5-18.5%, disappointing investors.

business.title: A $2.5 billion FII buying has domestic investors asking: Is foreign money shifting to India?
business.summary: Foreign investors bought Indian equities in July after significant selling periods. This return follows easing geopolitical concerns and improved global risk sentiment. India's growth story and diversified opportunities attract foreign capital for the long term. Analysts remain cautious, awaiting sustained inflows to confirm a trend. Crude oil prices and US interest rates will influence future foreign investment decisions.

business.title: Sensex falls 500 points, Nifty slips below 24,100 as US-Iran conflict escalates. What lies ahead?
business.summary: Indian stock markets edged lower on Tuesday, with major indices Sensex and Nifty falling. Escalating Middle East tensions contributed to rising oil prices, impacting investor sentiment negatively. Several prominent companies like HCL Technologies and Bajaj Finance saw significant share price drops. While some sectors like IT and Metal showed marginal gains, the overall market breadth remained bearish.

business.title: Grasim shares in focus after subsidiary acquires Solenergi Power from Shell in $1.8 billion deal
business.summary: Grasim Industries' renewable energy arm will acquire Solenergi Power from Shell. This acquisition significantly expands its clean energy portfolio to 9.4 GW. The deal, valued at Rs 17,200 crore, is expected to close by 2026. Funding will come from debt, equity, and an investment by Global Infrastructure Partners. The combined entity aims for 20 GW of renewable capacity within three years.

business.title: Q1 Showdown: Analysts pick top bets as ICICI Bank, HDFC, Axis, Kotak, Yes Bank gear up for results this week
business.summary: Five major private banks will announce their Q1 results this Saturday. Analysts offer mixed views on which banking stocks investors should consider. ICICI Bank is favored for its strong performance and reasonable valuation. HDFC Bank is seen as a defensive choice, while Axis Bank shows momentum. Kotak Mahindra Bank and Yes Bank present more selective investment opportunities.

Tensions in the Middle East deepened, leading to fresh strikes between the US and Iran. Indian equities also faced pressure, with the Nifty 50 index experiencing losses. The rupee reached its weakest point since late May due to these combined factors.

New ventures must also locate outside the Seoul metropolitan area.

This growth will be achieved through stronger free cash flows and internal accruals. Bansal Wire Industries is expanding into speciality wires and adopting AI-driven manufacturing. Improving return on capital and reducing debt remain central to its roadmap.

business.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | Equity, mutual funds, bonds or property: Tax rules every NRI should know: Ritu Shaktawat
business.summary: NRIs investing in India must understand tax rules and residential status.  Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements offer beneficial tax treatments for certain income streams.  Recent changes affect capital gains and mutual fund taxation significantly for investors.  Careful planning of investment exits and documentation is crucial for compliance.  Adhering to FEMA and RBI regulations ensures smooth repatriation of funds.

business.title: The great Adani trade is back with Rs 1.4 lakh crore bang! Why Adani Enterprises is Nifty’s hottest stock now

This funding will support business expansion, debt repayment, and strategic investments. Analysts expect strong earnings growth from its diverse and long-duration investment themes.
